text: Splitting a refurbished laptop into four payments made the purchase much easier to budget for.
Klarna appeared on the item page but disappeared when I reached checkout, so I had to use another payment method.
The payment schedule was clear in the Klarna app and the eBay order went through without any trouble.
I was not sure whether the seller would ship immediately or wait until all four payments were complete.
Pay in 4 worked smoothly on a collectible I had been saving for.
My purchase was declined and I did not understand whether the issue was the item, my account, or Klarna eligibility.
My return was approved, but I was confused about when the remaining installments would be adjusted.
The monthly financing rate made the item far more expensive than the price I first saw.
I liked having another way to pay at checkout without using my credit card for the full amount.
Klarna was not available when my cart included items with different delivery methods.
The redirect to Klarna and back to eBay was fast, and I could see every payment date before confirming.
I almost selected financing before noticing the interest rate, so the total cost should be more visible earlier.
The option was unavailable because the item shipped from outside the United States, but that rule was not obvious to me.
Being able to spread the cost helped me choose a certified refurbished phone instead of a cheaper unknown option.
I would use it again, but I want a clearer explanation of late fees and what happens if a payment fails.
The payment plan was easy, but getting help required switching between eBay and Klarna support.
